steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what leaf-vein architectures have. The sentence immediately lists distinct examples (branching venation of Ginkgo biloba, hierarchical netlike venation of Clematis reticulata, and others) and later refers to a range of adaptations. The word must mean something like 'variety' or 'diversity.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — multifariousness: means the quality of being numerous and varied. Fits exactly: the examples illustrate distinct architectural types, and 'range of adaptations' confirms variety is the concept. ✓
  • Choice B — obstinacy: means stubborn resistance to change. The passage describes multiple different forms, not a refusal to change. ✗
  • Choice C — entanglement: means a tangled or complicated state. The passage presents cleanly distinct architectural types, not confusion or tangling. ✗
  • Choice D — culpability: means blame or responsibility for a fault. No fault is involved; the passage is purely descriptive of adaptive diversity. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
